$schema: Fact Bank for Memory Benchmark — 100+ facts across 10 domains. All personas are fictional.
persona_note: Fictional test persona. No relation to real individuals.

facts.statement: I'm Kim Haneul. I run a startup and I'm a full-stack developer
These days I mainly code in TypeScript
I use Neovim as my editor
For frameworks I mainly use Next.js and FastAPI
I prefer dark mode and tab indentation
I live in Seongsu-dong
I only drink americanos. I don't like anything with milk
My younger sibling is Kim Bada. She works as a designer
I have an investor meeting next Thursday
I can't handle spicy food. I stick to mild stuff
I'm running Fedora. I like immutable OSes
I usually go running on weekends. I love running along the Han River
My company is an AI startup. We're building a personal AI app
I like having meetings in the morning. Afternoons are my focused coding time
I only use Git from the CLI. I don't use any GUI clients
I mainly use PostgreSQL for my database, and Redis for caching
I run CI/CD with GitHub Actions
I'm on GCP. Mainly Cloud Run and Cloud SQL
I use Podman instead of Docker. I like the rootless approach
I write tests with Vitest. It's faster than Jest
I use Biome as my formatter. Switched from Prettier
I use Ghostty as my terminal. The GPU acceleration is great
I use Fish shell. The autocompletion is so convenient
I have a dual monitor setup. Code on the left, browser on the right
I use a Happy Hacking Professional keyboard
The company name is Nextain. Founded it in 2024
We're a team of 5 including me. Running lean
I graduated from KAIST with a CS degree
My previous job was at Naver. I worked there for 3 years
I'm 32 years old. Born in '92
My mother is a teacher. She teaches at an elementary school
I have a dog. A golden retriever named Mungchi
My eyesight is pretty bad so I wear glasses. About -3.5
My blood type is A
My MBTI is INTJ
My favorite color is navy. I wear a lot of navy clothes too
I'm into jazz. I listen to Bill Evans a lot
I love sci-fi novels. Recently read Project Hail Mary
I'm a Christopher Nolan fan. Interstellar is my all-time favorite movie
Japan is my favorite travel destination. Kyoto is the best
My most important project right now is Naia OS. It's an AI desktop companion
The goal for this quarter is to launch beta. By end of April
extractable_facts: Goal: beta launch by end of April

Moved to Seoul in 8th grade. Lived in Seocho-gu
Didn't go to grad school because I wanted hands-on experience fast. Slightly regret the master's though
extractable_facts: No grad school: wanted practical experience

facts.statement: Nextain office is at Seongsu Social Venture Valley. A co-working space
Of the 5 team members, there's 1 designer, 1 frontend dev, and 2 backend devs
Nextain's vision is to give everyone their own personal AI
At Naver my salary was about 60 million won. Can't pay myself that much now
We got selected for TIPS. Receiving R&D funding with tech guarantee
My first hackathon was sophomore year. Didn't win but had fun
My usual intro is 'I'm Kim Haneul, CEO of Nextain. I build AI.'
extractable_facts: Intro: Kim Haneul, CEO of Nextain, builds AI

facts.statement: I keep an idea notebook in Notion. Over 300 ideas collected
When choosing business partners I value communication skills over technical ability
I believe resilience is the most important entrepreneurial trait
My professional role model isn't Elon Musk — it's Linus Torvalds
I care deeply about open source contribution. Naia OS is a public repo
extractable_facts: Values: open source, Naia OS public

facts.statement: Took 3 months off after leaving Naver. Traveled and brainstormed
Investment experience is 1 angel round. Total 50 million won
My strongest skill is frontend architecture
My weakness is design sense. That's why I need a designer
5-year goal is 100K MAU. Including global expansion
Used all my Naver severance as initial capital
extractable_facts: Initial capital: all from Naver severance

facts.statement: Completed a pre-startup training program. At K-Startup
Using pgvector extension for PostgreSQL. For embedding search
Infrastructure code managed with Terraform. Also considering Pulumi
Logging is structured. Using pino
No message queue yet, but planning to adopt BullMQ
API docs generated with TypeDoc instead of Swagger. Auto-generated from types
Monorepo managed with Turborepo. Nx is too heavy
Package manager is pnpm. Don't use npm or yarn
TypeScript strict mode. Any type in a PR gets rejected
CSS is Tailwind. Not a fan of CSS-in-JS
State management is Zustand. Redux has too much boilerplate
Error tracking with Sentry free plan. Paid is too expensive
Auth is NextAuth.js. OAuth2 based
Image optimization with Sharp library. Plus Next.js Image
WebSocket via Socket.io. For compatibility
Running a monolith, not microservices. Team is still small
LLM calls go through LangChain. For chain management
Vector DB is Qdrant. Pinecone is too expensive
No mobile app yet. When we do, it'll be React Native
E2E tests with Playwright. Faster and more stable than Cypress
GPU servers rented from RunPod. On-demand
Mainly using Claude for LLM. Better at coding than GPT
Code formatting rules also in .editorconfig. IDE-independent
Browser testing on Chrome mainly. Firefox occasionally
Planning canary deployments. Currently doing blue-green
Monitoring on Grafana Cloud free tier. Running 3 dashboards
Secrets in GCP Secret Manager. .env only for local
I use some Rust too. Tauri app backend is in Rust
Don't use Copilot. Using Claude Code instead. CLI is convenient
DNS is Cloudflare. CDN too
Domain registrar is Namecheap. Managing 3 domains
Linting handled by Biome along with formatting. Dropped ESLint
Git commits follow conventional commits
Branch strategy is GitHub Flow. Git Flow is too complex
Tauri app uses WebKitGTK. Uses less memory than Electron
Embedding model is BGE-M3. Great multilingual support
Local LLM via ollama. Mainly for testing
Profiling with Chrome DevTools Performance tab. No extra tools
HTTP client is ky. Lighter than axios
Form management with React Hook Form. Formik is too heavy
ORM is Drizzle. Switched from Prisma for performance
Avatar rendering with Three.js. WebGL based
Testing Coqui TTS for speech synthesis. Want to run locally
STT is Whisper. The faster-whisper version
Date library is dayjs. moment is deprecated
Table component is TanStack Table. Supports virtual scrolling
Icons are Lucide React. Open source and clean
Environment variable validation with zod. Type-safe
Mouse is Logitech MX Master 3S. Great for programming
Dev machine runs Linux. No Mac. I prefer the freedom
Laptop is a Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon. Great Linux compatibility
My favorite jazz bar is 'All That Jazz' in Jongno. Great atmosphere
extractable_facts: Jazz bar: All That Jazz, Jongno

facts.statement: I prefer medium roast for coffee beans. Dark is too bitter
I skip breakfast. Sort of intermittent fasting
I like Korean food for lunch. Love soup dishes. Doenjang-jjigae is the best
I eat light dinners. Salad or sandwiches
I like margherita pizza. Not a fan of heavy toppings
